preamble: Nasal consonants can weaken and lose their occlusion, leaving behind a palatal glide in some language varieties.

examples.note: Weakening of nasals to glides, especially in coda positions.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
